avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads especially above approximately 2500 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
In addition the hard wind slabs in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered in some cases still. In particular at low and intermediate altitudes and in steep rocky terrain individual mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the old snowpack above approximately 2500 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Monday: In some localities 5 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1600 m.

In particular steep sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.

Especially steep west, north and northeast facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular above approximately 2400 m: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in some place by winter sport participants. This applies in particular on very steep west, north and northeast facing slopes.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural avalanche activity will decrease.
In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in steep terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Monday: In some localities 5 to 15 cm of snow, and up to 20 cm in some localities, has fallen since Monday above approximately 1600 m.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Weak layers exist in the snowpack especially on west, north and northeast facing slopes.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads especially above approximately 2500 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to a loss of strength within the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the old snowpack above approximately 2500 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Monday: In some localities up to 10 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1600 m.

Especially steep sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The old snowpack is hard and its surface has a strong melt-freeze crust.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to softening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
Especially steep west, north and northeast facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In these regions only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

avalancheActivity.comment: The snowpack will be in most cases well bonded. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before. As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural avalanche activity will decrease. In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
avalancheActivity.highlights: A mostly favourable avalanche situation will prevail. Decrease in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the temperature drops.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before. Here a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. This applies at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on extremely steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural avalanche activity will decrease.
In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack will be quite stable. Decrease in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the temperature drops.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Monday: In some localities up to 10 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1600 m.

In particular low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.
